basic_details.job_title: CARI RRAP Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for Senior Research Fellow (Ayu)/ Yoga Therapist Posts
basic_details.short_information: Raja Ramdeo Anandilal Podar (CARI RRAP) Senior Research Fellow (Ayu) / Yoga Therapist Notification 2026 has been released under Advertisement No. 02/2026-27 on the official website of CARI RRAP. A total of 5 vacancies have been announced through the CARI RRAP Senior Research Fellow (Ayu) / Yoga Therapist Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 35 years Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC candidates as per Government of India orders.

qualification.education_qualification: BAMS, MD (Ayurveda), Degree/Diploma in Yoga/Swasthavritta, Degree in Yoga or Yoga & Naturopathy with 55% marks, Level II (Therapy level) Certificate of YCB
qualification.details: Senior Research Fellow (Ayu): BAMS degree from a recognized University. Desirable: MD (Ayurveda) with previous research experience in Ministry of AYUSH, ICMR, CSIR, DST funded project; Skill of Drafting/editing of scientific documents; Publication in peer-reviewed journals; Knowledge of MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint. OR BAMS degree from a recognized University. Desirable: MD (Ayurveda), Degree/Diploma/Certificate in Yoga/Swasthavritta with previous yoga-based research experience in Ministry of AYUSH, ICMR, DST funded project; Skills of drafting & editing of scientific documents; Publication in peer-reviewed journals; Knowledge of MS Office. OR BAMS degree from a recognized University. Desirable: MD (Ayurveda) with previous research experience; Skills of drafting & editing of scientific documents; Publication in peer-reviewed journals; Knowledge of computer applications. Yoga Therapist: Degree in Yoga or Yoga & Naturopathy with minimum 55% marks and two years experience in Yoga Therapy OR Any Graduate with 55% marks and one year Diploma in Yoga with five years practical experience; Level II (Therapy level) Certificate of YCB; Demonstration skills in Yogic practices. Desirable: Working experience in traditional Yoga Therapy; Practical ability to perform Yogic practices; Knowledge of computer operations; Working knowledge of Hindi, English and Sanskrit; Working experience in research projects related to Yoga Therapy.

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 42,000 + HRA (30%) per month (consolidated) for SRF (Ayu), Rs. 38,000 per month (consolidated) for Yoga Therapist
salary_details.details: Senior Research Fellow (Ayu): Rs. 42,000 + HRA (30%) per month (consolidated) Yoga Therapist: Rs. 38,000 per month (consolidated)

selection_process: Walk-in Interview Document Verification Skill Test (for Yoga Therapist)

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the CARI RRAP recruitment?
faq.answer: The recruitment is through a walk-in interview, and the interview is scheduled for 16 June 2026. Candidates should report to the venue on this date.

faq.question: Q2. What is the total number of vacancies announced by CARI RRAP?
faq.answer: CARI RRAP has announced a total of 5 vacancies for the posts of Senior Research Fellow (Ayu) and Yoga Therapist.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Senior Research Fellow (Ayu) post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a BAMS degree from a recognized University. An MD (Ayurveda) is desirable, along with research experience and computer skills.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the advertised posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all the advertised posts is 35 years. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST/OBC candidates as per government norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary offered for the Yoga Therapist position?
faq.answer: The selected candidate for the Yoga Therapist post will receive a consolidated salary of Rs. 38,000 per month.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee required for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. Where will the walk-in interview be conducted?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be conducted in Mumbai, Maharashtra, at the CARI RRAP facility. The exact address is provided in the official notification.

faq.question: Q8. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must carry all original documents, self-attested photocopies of these documents, and recent passport-size photographs to the interview venue.

faq.question: Q9. What is the mode of selection for these posts?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a Walk-in Interview, Document Verification, and a Skill Test specifically for the Yoga Therapist position.

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent government job?
faq.answer: No, these positions are contractual in nature, offering a fixed tenure as per the terms of the appointment.
